error_signature: User is missing a scope required to perform this action
title: Missing scope error when deactivating workflows
symptom: When attempting to deactivate a workflow using the Owner account, n8n returns an error stating 'User is missing a scope required to perform this action'. This prevents the workflow from being deactivated.
not_this_if: Using the API key of the workflow's creator
Workflow is not in a private project
root_cause: The error occurs due to changes in how permissions are handled in n8n version 2.x. The Owner account may not have the necessary scopes enabled for certain actions, such as deactivating workflows, especially if the workflow is part of a private project.
fix_steps: Ensure the Owner's API key has 'workflow:update' and 'workflow:unpublish' scopes enabled
If the workflow is in a private project, add the Owner as a collaborator with edit rights
Regenerate the API key with all permissions checked if issues persist
agent_instruction: Check and update the API key scopes for the Owner account and ensure proper project sharing settings.
